name: supermedicine
description: Standalone modular medical research assistant with optional OpenCode and Claude Code adapter entry files

ai_provider.used_by: Claude Code optional invocation context through SuperMedicine runtime configuration

limitations: The standalone SuperMedicine core does not require Claude Code.
Local Claude Code CLI is optional and required only for claude.invoke.
OpenAI/Anthropic provider configuration is supplied by installer/runtime/project config and must not be embedded as plaintext credentials.
No native Claude Code skill loading or native subagent dispatch is implemented.

opencode.native_skill_loading: skill documents provided; adapter can load local declared files
limitations: The standalone SuperMedicine core does not require OpenCode.
Only SuperMedicine is user-facing; alpha/beta/gamma/delta are internal role contexts.
AI provider configuration supports OpenAI and Anthropic formats, custom BaseURL values, and redacted secret handling through installer/runtime/project config sources.
Task dispatch needs an injected SuperMedicine orchestrator/runtime bridge; otherwise it returns a degraded context result.

install_completeness_model.core_default: Python package and CLI run independently of assistant platforms.
install_completeness_model.platform_add_ons: OpenCode and Claude Code entries are optional metadata/adapter surfaces packaged under adapters/.
install_completeness_model.truthfulness_boundary: Adapter manifests declare only implemented local files, metadata, tool mappings, and degraded/unavailable states.

install_steps.notes: Optional non-interactive LLM injection: --provider openai|anthropic --base-url <url> --api-key <placeholder-or-secret> --model <model>, or SM_LLM_PROVIDER/SM_LLM_BASE_URL/SM_LLM_API_KEY/SM_LLM_MODEL and provider API key env vars. Use fake placeholders in shared docs; do not commit real keys. Use --interactive to prompt. Installer output redacts API keys.

Safe local cleanup entry. By default it removes only project-owned .supermedicine/runtime artifacts and installer-created platform target paths recorded in .supermedicine/install-record.json or passed explicitly; it does not remove the source repository or pip package.

uninstall.ownership_rule: Project-owned files are canonical SuperMedicine local directories/files inside the selected project root (.supermedicine, runtime artifacts, workspaces) plus installer-created platform target paths explicitly recorded in .supermedicine/install-record.json or passed via --target. Platform directories such as .opencode, .claude, and superpowers are never unconditional delete candidates. Outside-project paths, the source repository root itself, source files, and unrecorded user-owned global platform configuration are skipped.
opencode_artifacts: installer-recorded .opencode plugin, agent, skill, or capability files owned by SuperMedicine
